info.title: SurgeGraph REST Facade
info.description: Thin REST facade over the SurgeGraph MCP tool surface. One operation per MCP tool, wire-compatible with the OAuth 2.1 bearer tokens issued by https://mcp.surgegraph.io. Generated by `bun run --filter @apps/mcp export:openapi`.

tags.name: account
tags.description: Quota usage, credit balance, and team membership.

tags.name: ai_visibility
tags.description: Read and manage AI Visibility prompts, citations, topics, sentiment, traffic, and opportunities.

tags.name: api_keys
tags.description: List, create, update, and delete OpenAI / Gemini / Anthropic API keys.

tags.name: cms
tags.description: Inspect WordPress integrations, categories, authors, and publish documents to CMS.

tags.name: content
tags.description: List, read, create, update, and delete writer documents, optimized documents, and authors.

tags.name: content_vision
tags.description: Inspect content vision settings, gallery, and create images.

tags.name: knowledge_library
tags.description: List, create, and delete knowledge libraries and their documents.

tags.name: misc
tags.description: Miscellaneous lookups (locations, languages, writer models, brand mentions).

post.summary: Create a new prompt to AI Visibility tracking
post.description: Create a new prompt to AI Visibility tracking. Prompts are tracked across AI answer engines to monitor brand visibility. Topics and tags are resolved by name and auto-created if they do not exist. Note: tag names are lowercased on write and lookup is case-insensitive — "My-Tag" and "my-tag" resolve to the same tag.

prompt.description: The prompt text to track (e.g. "What is the best SEO tool?")

topicName.description: Topic name to associate the prompt with. Will be created if it does not exist.

tags.description: Tag names to assign. Tags will be created if they do not exist. Tag names are lowercased on write and lookup is case-insensitive.

403.description: Token is valid but the user lacks the required permission or plan feature.

post.summary: Create an LLM provider API key for the organization
post.description: Create an LLM provider API key for the organization. The key is validated against the provider before being stored. Optionally assign it to one or more projects. Note: each project can only be assigned to one API key per provider — assigning to a project that already has a key of the same provider will fail with a conflict.

key.description: Raw API key (will be validated against the provider and stored encrypted)

projectIds.description: Project IDs to assign this key to

200.description: Tool result. Schema is the raw JSON payload the tool produced.

400.description: Request body failed validation against the tool input schema.

401.description: Missing or invalid OAuth 2.1 bearer token.

403.description: Token is valid but the user lacks the required permission or plan feature.

post.summary: Bulk create multiple AI-written documents in a single batch (max 50)
post.description:
Bulk create multiple AI-written documents in a single batch (max 50). Each article needs a prompt; all other settings are shared.
Only 'general' and 'listicle' article types are supported for bulk generation. Requires a BYOK API key assigned to the project for the chosen model's provider (e.g. OpenAI BYOK for gpt-* models, Gemini BYOK for gemini-* models, Anthropic BYOK for claude-* models).
Articles are generated asynchronously with 30-second staggered delays between each.

IMPORTANT — webhook setup is REQUIRED for OpenAI bulk generation. Before calling this tool with an OpenAI (gpt-*) model, you MUST first ask the user to confirm they have added SurgeGraph's webhook URL "https://webhook-v2-935801613060.us-central1.run.app/openai" to their OpenAI organization webhook settings (https://platform.openai.com/settings/organization/webhooks). Without this webhook, OpenAI cannot deliver the generated articles back to SurgeGraph and the batch will silently never complete. Set 'webhookAcknowledged: true' ONLY after the user explicitly confirms; if they have not, instruct them to add the webhook first and do not proceed.

keyword.description: Primary keyword if different from prompt

model.description: AI model ID for all articles

languageCode.description: Language code (default: en). Use get_languages to discover valid values.

jobName.description: Batch job name (default: auto-generated with timestamp)

webhookAcknowledged.description: REQUIRED for OpenAI (gpt-*) models. Set to true ONLY after the user has explicitly confirmed they have added SurgeGraph's webhook URL "https://webhook-v2-935801613060.us-central1.run.app/openai" to their OpenAI organization webhook settings. If the user has not confirmed, ask them first — do not assume or default to true. For non-OpenAI providers (Gemini, Anthropic), set to true.

200.description: Tool result. Schema is the raw JSON payload the tool produced.

post.summary: Create and generate an AI-written document
post.description:
Create and generate an AI-written document. Only "prompt" is required — all other fields have sensible defaults.
Use get_writer_models to see available models, get_locations for location codes, and get_languages for language codes.
The document will be generated asynchronously. Use get_document to check its status.

Article-type-specific fields:
- articleType="review": pass `review.productName` (required) and optional `review.productUrl`.
- articleType="comparison": pass `comparison.custom` with at least 2 entries that have `productName`.
- articleType="roundup": pass `roundup.type` (serp | amazon | custom). For "amazon" you may pass `roundup.amazonProductSort`. For "custom" pass `roundup.custom`.
- articleType="listicle": optionally pass `listicle.type` (serp | custom). For "custom" pass `listicle.custom` as a newline-separated list of items.
Product URLs must contain http:// or https://.

prompt.description: Main topic or keyword for the article (e.g. "best project management tools 2025")

model.description: AI model ID. Use get_writer_models to see options. Default: gpt-5.4

title.description: Custom article title. If omitted, AI generates one

contentGoal.description: Content objective or goal for the article

locationCode.description: Location code (default: 2840 for US). Use get_locations

languageCode.description: Language code (default: en). Use get_languages to discover valid values.

skipOutlineReview.description: Skip outline review and start writing immediately (default: true)

outline.description: Custom outline text. Each line is a heading. Prefix with H1-H6 tag (e.g. "H2: Introduction"). Default: H2

knowledgeLibraryIds.description: Knowledge library IDs to use as context

knowledgeCreditChoice.description: How to pay the knowledge surcharge: "credit" deducts from the credit balance (default), "byok" requires an active project OpenAI key

productName.description: Product name for the review (required when articleType="review")

type.description: serp: SERP-derived; amazon: Amazon search; custom: caller-provided products
serp: derive items from SERP; custom: caller-provided items

custom.description: Newline-separated list of items when type="custom"

amazonAffiliateId.description: Amazon affiliate ID for review/comparison/roundup with Amazon URLs

post.summary: Start a domain research for topic coverage analysis
post.description:
Start a domain research for topic coverage analysis. Analyzes a domain to discover relevant topics. Results are cached for 30 days.
Returns a research ID. The research runs asynchronously — use get_domain_research to check progress and fetch extracted topics, or list_domain_researches to see all entries.

languageCode.description: Language code (default: en). Use get_languages to discover valid values.

post.summary: Generate an AI image and store it in the Content Vision gallery
post.description:
Generate an AI image and store it in the Content Vision gallery. Use get_content_vision_settings first to see available image types and styles for the project.
Returns the generated image URL and metadata.

prompt.description: Image generation prompt describing what to create

imageTypeId.description: Image type ID (e.g. infographic, photography). Use get_content_vision_settings to see available types

styleId.description: Style ID for the image. Use get_content_vision_settings to see available styles per type

negativePrompt.description: What NOT to include in the image

isFeaturedImage.description: Whether this is a featured/hero image

textSensitive.description: Whether to be text-sensitive in generation

post.summary: Create a text document to a knowledge library
post.description: Create a text document to a knowledge library. The document content will be indexed for use as AI writing context. Max 40,000 words. Defaults to charging the indexing surcharge in credits; set creditChoice="byok" to skip the surcharge using an OpenAI key assigned to the project.

libraryId.description: Knowledge library ID to add the document to

creditChoice.description: How to pay for indexing: "credit" deducts the surcharge from credits (default), "byok" uses the project OpenAI key

post.summary: Create a content-optimized document from an existing WordPress post
post.description:
Create a content-optimized document from an existing WordPress post. The system fetches the post content via wpPostId, then analyzes and rewrites it for better AEO/SEO performance.
Requires a WordPress integration to be configured for the project. The URL and title can be auto-resolved from the WordPress post if not provided.
Use get_writer_models for available models and get_locations/get_languages for codes.

url.description: URL of the content to optimize. If omitted, the URL is retrieved from the WordPress post.

languageCode.description: Language code (default: en). Use get_languages to discover valid values.

wpPostId.description: WordPress post ID for the content being optimized

post.summary: Start a topic research for topic coverage analysis
post.description:
Start a topic research for topic coverage analysis. Analyzes a specific topic to discover subtopics, related keywords, and content gaps. Results are cached for 30 days.
Returns a research ID. Use list_topic_researches to see all researches and get_topic_research to fetch a specific result (use get_topic_map for the project-wide coverage map).
Use get_locations for location codes and get_languages for language codes.

locationCode.description: Location code (default: 2840 for US). Use get_locations

languageCode.description: Language code (default: en). Use get_languages to discover valid values.

post.summary: Expand an existing topic research by generating micro topics under one or more macro topics
post.description:
Expand an existing topic research by generating micro topics under one or more macro topics. Each macro topic generates its own batch of micro topics in the background.
Consumes TOPIC_RESEARCH_EXPANSION credits — one credit per macro ID that is newly expanded (macro IDs already expanded are skipped and do not consume credit).
Use get_topic_research with a researchId to list macro topic IDs (nodes where type === "macro"). Results stream in asynchronously — poll get_topic_research to see new micro topics appear under each macro.

projectId.description: Project ID that owns the research

researchId.description: Topic research ID to expand (must belong to projectId)

macroIds.description: Macro topic node IDs to expand into micro topics. Get them from get_topic_research → topics[].id where type is "macro".

post.summary: Delete a SurgeGraph document by ID
post.description: Delete a SurgeGraph document by ID. Use get_writer_documents or get_optimized_documents to find document IDs. Defaults to "trash" (soft delete) — the document is hidden from list/optimizer views but recoverable by setting status back. Pass mode="permanent" to hard-delete the row irrecoverably.

mode.description: "trash" (default) flips document.status to "trash" so it can be restored. "permanent" hard-deletes the row.

post.summary: Retrieve prompts configured for AI Visibility tracking in a project
post.description: Retrieve prompts configured for AI Visibility tracking in a project. Paginated: defaults to page 1 with pageSize 50 (max 200). Provide startDate + endDate to attach averageVisibility and shareOfVoice for the project's tracked brand. Use sortBy="averageVisibility" with sortOrder="asc" to surface the worst-performing prompts. Returns a `{ data, meta }` envelope.

startDate.description: Inclusive start date for visibility metrics (ISO 8601). Required together with endDate to enable averageVisibility/shareOfVoice and metric-based sorting.

endDate.description: Inclusive end date for visibility metrics (ISO 8601). Required together with startDate.

sortBy.description: Sort key. averageVisibility / shareOfVoice require startDate + endDate.

post.summary: Publish a SurgeGraph document to the CMS connected to its project (currently WordPress)
post.description:
Publish a SurgeGraph document to the CMS connected to its project (currently WordPress). Creates a new post on the target site with the document's title, content, meta description, schema markup, featured image, and inline images. Product shortcodes are enriched with product metadata before publish.
The target integration is derived from the document's project. If the project has no CMS integration connected, this tool fails — call update_project_cms_integration first to connect one. Use get_wordpress_categories to look up categoryIds, get_wordpress_authors to look up authorId. If authorId is omitted, the document's mapped WordPress author is used.

categoryIds.description: WordPress category IDs to assign to the post. Use get_wordpress_categories to find IDs.

authorId.description: WordPress author ID override. If omitted, falls back to the document's mapped WP author. Use get_wordpress_authors to find IDs.

200.description: Tool result. Schema is the raw JSON payload the tool produced.

400.description: Request body failed validation against the tool input schema.

401.description: Missing or invalid OAuth 2.1 bearer token.

403.description: Token is valid but the user lacks the required permission or plan feature.

post.summary: Update an existing AI Visibility prompt
post.description: Update an existing AI Visibility prompt. Can modify the prompt text, topic, and tags. Topics and tags are resolved by name and auto-created if they do not exist. Use get_ai_visibility_prompts to get prompt IDs. Note: tag names are lowercased on write and lookup is case-insensitive — "My-Tag" and "my-tag" resolve to the same tag.

topicName.description: Topic name to associate with. Will be created if it does not exist.

addTags.description: Tag names to add. Tags will be created if they do not exist. Tag names are lowercased on write and lookup is case-insensitive.

removeTags.description: Tag names to remove from this prompt. Matching is case-insensitive against the stored (lowercased) tag name.

200.description: Tool result. Schema is the raw JSON payload the tool produced.

400.description: Request body failed validation against the tool input schema.

401.description: Missing or invalid OAuth 2.1 bearer token.

403.description: Token is valid but the user lacks the required permission or plan feature.

post.summary: Update an existing API key
post.description: Update an existing API key. Can change the raw key, active status, label, or project assignments. Passing `projectIds` replaces the full list of assigned projects (pass `[]` to unassign all). Each project can only be assigned to one key per provider — conflicts throw an error. Use get_openai_keys / get_gemini_keys / get_anthropic_keys to find the numeric key ID.

key.description: New raw API key (will be re-validated against the provider). Omit to keep the existing key.

projectIds.description: Replaces the full set of assigned projects. Pass [] to unassign all. Omit to leave assignments unchanged.

200.description: Tool result. Schema is the raw JSON payload the tool produced.

400.description: Request body failed validation against the tool input schema.

401.description: Missing or invalid OAuth 2.1 bearer token.

403.description: Token is valid but the user lacks the required permission or plan feature.

post.summary: Update an existing SurgeGraph document's basic fields: title, content (HTML body), meta description, and/or schema markup
post.description:
Update an existing SurgeGraph document's basic fields: title, content (HTML body), meta description, and/or schema markup. Records a history snapshot automatically when title or content changes. For publishing to WordPress use publish_document_to_cms.
Scope is intentionally narrow — outline, author, writing settings, and image generation are not editable via this tool. Use get_document to inspect the current state before editing. When 'mode' is 'append', the provided content is concatenated to the existing body instead of replacing it.

content.description: HTML body content. Replaces the existing content unless mode is "append".

schemaMarkup.description: Structured data (JSON-LD object or array) stored under document_meta["schema-markup"]

mode.description: How to apply the content field. "replace" overwrites the body (default); "append" concatenates onto the current body. Only affects the content field.

200.description: Tool result. Schema is the raw JSON payload the tool produced.

400.description: Request body failed validation against the tool input schema.

401.description: Missing or invalid OAuth 2.1 bearer token.

403.description: Token is valid but the user lacks the required permission or plan feature.

post.description: Connect or change the CMS integration for a project (currently WordPress only). Use get_wordpress_integrations to find the integration ID. After this is set, publish_document_to_cms will publish documents from this project to that integration. Replaces any existing connection on the project.

projectId.description: Project ID to connect the CMS integration to

get.description: Returns the name and description of every tool exposed on the REST facade. Mirrors the MCP tool list.

200.description: Tool index.

bearerAuth.description: OAuth 2.1 access token issued by https://mcp.surgegraph.io/token. Same token format and validation as the /mcp endpoint. The CLI obtains a token via the `auth login` browser flow (Authorization Code + PKCE + Dynamic Client Registration).

x-auth-vars.name: SURGEGRAPH_TOKEN
x-auth-vars.description: OAuth 2.1 bearer token issued by https://mcp.surgegraph.io/token. Populated by 'surgegraph-pp-cli auth login --browser' (Authorization Code + PKCE + Dynamic Client Registration). Refresh token rotation honored.
